reference: The code has a data race: multiple goroutines write to the shared map m without synchronization, and Go maps are not safe for concurrent writes. Fix by guarding m with a sync.Mutex (or sync.RWMutex), or use sync.Map. Also, the goroutine's result is not awaited, so callers cannot know when the increment completes.
prediction: There is a data race: the goroutines all write to the package-level map m concurrently with no synchronization, and Go maps are not safe for concurrent writes (this will panic under the race detector or at runtime). Guard m with a sync.Mutex around the m[k]++, or switch to sync.Map. The unsynchronized goroutine also makes completion unobservable; consider a sync.WaitGroup.
